body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` **Triggered by:** commit ded185d2b2695dd7b960b8ede052463e73d6808a (PR #58177) by @jorgerangel-msft ## What Changed PR #58177 introduced four new resource-oriented sub-client classes as part of a major API refactoring of the Conversations Authoring SDK: - **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — project-level operations (create, delete, import, export, train, swap deployments, assign/unassign resources, etc.) - **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deployment-level operations (deploy, get, delete, delete from resources, etc.) - **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — trained model operations - **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — exported model operations The main `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client was significantly refactored (3,591 deletions, 16 additions), and collection result classes were renamed. All sample test files were updated to use the new sub-client pattern. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** — the `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` sections are all blank despite the introduction of four new sub-clients and a major refactoring of the primary client - **README `Key concepts` section does not mention the new sub-clients** — the section only references `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` as the primary client; the new resource-oriented sub-client hierarchy (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`) is not described - **README SDK version table is out of date** — the table only includes entries through `1.0.0-beta.3`; `1.0.0-beta.4` is missing along with its supported API versions - **No samples exist for the new sub-clients** — all existing samples still use the flat `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` pattern; there are no samples demonstrating how to obtain and use the new sub-clients
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the existing `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` section to document the new sub-clients and breaking changes: ```markdown ## 1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added new resource-oriented sub-clients for more granular API access: - `ConversationAuthoringProject` — provides project-level operations including create, get, delete, import, export, train, cancel training, swap deployments, assign/unassign project resources, and resource status polling. - `ConversationAuthoringDeployment` — provides deployment-level operations including deploy project, get deployment, delete deployment, delete deployment from resources, and resource deletion status polling. - `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel` — provides trained model operations including get, delete, load snapshot, and model evaluation. - `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel` — provides exported model operations. ### Breaking Changes - The primary `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client has been significantly refactored. Many operations previously available directly on `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` are now accessed via the new sub-clients (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`). - Collection result classes for trained model evaluation results have been renamed for consistency. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` **A) Update the SDK version table** (in the "Install the package" section) to add the `1.0.0-beta.4` row. Locate this table: ```markdown |SDK version |Supported API version of service |-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------- |1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) ``` Replace with: ```markdown |SDK version |Supported API version of service |-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------- |1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) |1.0.0-beta.4 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default) ``` > **Note:** Verify the exact supported API versions for `1.0.0-beta.4` by checking `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/src/Generated/ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.cs` (or equivalent options class) before finalizing. **B) Update the `Key concepts` → `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client` section** to describe the sub-client architecture. Replace the existing paragraph with: ```markdown ### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client The [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the top-level entry point for the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations and serves as the factory for the following resource-oriented sub-clients: - **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — create, get, delete, import, export, and train conversation projects; assign and unassign project resources; swap deployments. - **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deploy projects, get and delete deployments, and manage deployment resource assignments. - **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — get and delete trained models; load snapshots; retrieve evaluation summaries and results. - **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — manage exported model snapshots. Obtain sub-client instances from the primary client (check the generated client for the exact factory method names, e.g., `GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient()`). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new sample test file demonstrating the new sub-client pattern: **Test file path:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample24_ConversationsAuthoring_SubClientArchitecture.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure; using Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring.Tests.Samples { public partial class ConversationsAuthoringClientLiveTest : RecordedTestBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] public void Sample24_SubClientArchitecture() { #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_GetSubClients Uri endpoint = new Uri("{endpoint}"); AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential("{api-key}"); ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential); // Obtain resource-oriented sub-clients from the primary client ConversationAuthoringProject projectClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient(); ConversationAuthoringDeployment deploymentClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringDeploymentClient(); ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel trainedModelClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringTrainedModelClient(); #endregion } } } ``` > **Note:** Check the generated `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring.cs` for the exact factory method names for obtaining sub-clients before finalizing the snippet. **README location:** Insert a new entry in the `Examples` section listing the new sample, and add a snippet placeholder right after the `Key concepts` sub-client architecture description: ```C# Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_GetSubClients ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README has all required sections. The following items need updating: 1. **Getting started → Install the package**: Update the SDK version table to include `1.0.0-beta.4` (see Step 1A above). 2. **Key concepts → ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client**: Expand to describe the sub-client hierarchy (see Step 1B above). 3. **Examples**: Add a link to the new `Sample24` demonstrating how to obtain sub-clients. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Conversations Authoring] Docs: Document new sub-client architecture in README and CHANGELOG
